title: Laggera oloptera
taxonomy: Laggera oloptera is a species in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Laggera, which is a group of flowering plants. The species Laggera oloptera is native to tropical regions and is often classified as a shrub or herbaceous plant. Laggera oloptera is closely related to other species in the genus, such as Laggera crispata and Laggera aurita. The classification of Laggera oloptera is also supported by its morphological and anatomical characteristics, including its leaf and flower structures.

intro: Laggera oloptera, also known as African bush tea or bush tomato, is a perennial shrub native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Laggera oloptera is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects.
